title: 3 Establishing Policies for Client Centre User Management
body_text:
3	Establishing Policies for Client Centre User Management
3.1	Policy on Restricting Access to Applications on the Client Centre
The Client Centre is the gateway to high security applications (e.g., the Wires application) that may be susceptible to employee fraud. We recommend that you segregate duties and restrict administrative access to applications on the Client Centre.
Your financial institution is responsible for designating its own CPS Admin Security Officers. Practice a high level of due diligence when appointing CPS Admin Security Officers. Make sure that the applications assigned to each CPS Admin Security Officer and each Client Centre User reflect current job responsibilities and authorization levels.
3.2	Policy on Internal Controls to User Management and Client Centre User Management
Your financial institution is responsible for employing security practices and establishing internal controls to monitor Client Centre Users. It is recommended that internal controls and procedures relating to the use of applications on the Client Centre are established to prevent or detect errors promptly. Your operations staff and external and internal auditors should pay particular attention to internal controls for managing Client Centre Users.
Policies may include but arenât limited to:
Ensuring that Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers have their own unique user IDs
Important
:	Shared user IDs must be avoided.
Deleting Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers who
â
no longer require CPS Admin or Client Centre access,
â
are no longer employees of your financial institution, or
â
are inactive for one year or more.
Note:	Deleting a Client Centre user ID is permanent. The deleted User ID canât be used again.
Ensuring that Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers are reviewed on a regular basis and that action, if required, is taken to update or delete user profiles on CPS Admin
If an existing user hasnât accessed the Client Centre for 13 months, Central 1 will disable the userâs account. After a further 90 days, Central 1 will delete the userâs account. A deleted account can be recovered for up to 90 days.
If the Client Centre User is new and doesnât access the Client Centre or any application via the Client Centre within 15 days following the account creation date, Central 1 will disable the account.
3.3	Policy on Passwords
Secure passwords for Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers are necessary to prevent unauthorized access to the computer network. A password breach could result in any of the following:
Theft of data or confidential information
Destruction of network systems
Deletion of data
Employee impersonation
Fraud
Breach of member confidentiality
Damage to the reputation of the financial institution if the damage done by the password breach is significant
We recommend that your financial institution establish policies to prevent these risks. You can include the following guidelines in your policies:
One-time passwords must be
â
unique for each Client Centre User and CPS Admin Security Officer,
â
communicated in a secure and confidential manner, and
â
changed by the Client Centre User or CPS Admin Security Officer as quickly as possible.
Characteristics of Secure Passwords are as follows:
â
Must be a minimum of eight characters in length
â
Can contain alphanumeric and special characters, including capital letters
â
Canât be the same password used as one of the last six passwords
Passwords must
not
contain common acronyms, including credit union name abbreviations, and words or numbers that have personal significance to the end user, such as birth dates or the names of relatives and pets.
Passwords should be specific to the organization and shouldnât be used as passwords on websites or any other computer systems. It is recommended to use a passphrase as these are harder to guess (e.g., âdeskchairphonemugâ or âI LOVE ice Cream dayâ).
Passwords are checked against a list of known insecure passwords to prevent use of passwords that are known to have been exposed or shared from internet data breaches. This means that some password changes will fail even though the new password meets the structure requirements. If this occurs, the user should select a new password or use a passphrase instead.
Rules for Regular Password Updates
Passwords must be renewed every six months. Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers should avoid reusing previous passwords. The last six passwords canât be reused.
Rules for Secure Password Use
To prevent a password breach, it is recommended that you establish rules regarding password use:
Passwords should be memorized and not written down.
Users shouldnât give their password to a co-worker for any reason. This includes network operations and Help Desk staff.
Each user must have their own login ID and password. Shared login IDs and passwords must be avoided.
Users should make sure that no one is watching when they enter their password.
Reporting of Password Breaches
If Client Centre Users or CPS Admin Security Officers think that their password has been compromised, they must
report the incident to their immediate supervisor, and
change the password immediately.
Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers must be made aware that they are responsible for their passwords, and that they will be held partly responsible for any operational misuse that occurs under their account.
END
